user_query:
I’ve recorded an IMU calibration session covering a wide range of orientations. Please calibrate the magnetometer from the session and document your work.

Inputs:
- Read input/imu_session.csv (columns: ts, ax, ay, az, gx, gy, gz, mx, my, mz). Use the data as-is.

Deliverables (write all files under output/):
1) output/calibration.json — JSON with numeric fields:
   { "Sm": [[...],[...],[...]], "h": [h1,h2,h3] }
   where Sm is a 3x3 soft-iron matrix and h is a 3-element hard-iron offset vector.
2) output/calibrated_mags.csv — the first 200 calibrated magnetometer samples using your Sm and h, with header "mx,my,mz" (no extra columns). Calibrate via: m_cal = Sm @ (m_raw - h).
3) output/metrics.json — before/after metrics computed over the entire session, with these fields:
   - raw_norm_mean, raw_norm_std
   - cal_norm_mean, cal_norm_std
   - frac_within_0p9_1p1_before, frac_within_0p9_1p1_after
   Norms are Euclidean norms of (mx,my,mz) before and after calibration.
4) output/report.md — a concise technical report explaining your calibration approach, assumptions, and sanity checks. Include a short code snippet showing how to apply Sm and h to new magnetometer measurements.
5) output/README.md — quick instructions on how to reproduce your metrics from the inputs and your calibration parameters.

Requirements:
- Do not hardcode constants; compute from the data.
- Ensure all arrays/matrices are numeric (no NaNs/strings) and shapes are correct.
- Validate your calibration by showing the post-calibration magnetometer norms are closer to a unit sphere than the raw data (lower std and a higher fraction within [0.9, 1.1]).
- Use sensible tolerances and clearly state any assumptions in the report.
